observation.summary: Full-access runtime harness rules were prose while Codex launch shape, override dropping, and provider-default denial lived across adapter tests and provider validation fixtures.

hypothesis.summary: A manifest can generate the reader doc and prove daemon-owned Codex full-access launch, caller override rejection, and absent permission-profile generation from source references.

execution.summary: Run fullaccessharness to check generated docs, source checks, and absent unsafe/default policy surfaces.

evaluation.summary: The verifier rejects generated doc drift, missing Codex adapter evidence, missing provider validation matrix claims, permission profile generation, and default host unsafe bypass allowances.

retrospective.summary: Full-access harness knowledge becomes executable evidence instead of a manually edited security note.

evidence.proves: full-access harness manifest matches generated docs, source evidence, and absent unsafe/default policy surfaces
public CI uploads full-access harness evidence
